name: api-and-interface-design
description: Contract-first API design with Hyrum's Law + One-Version Rule + typed errors

positive_triggers.input: Design a REST API for our user management module
positive_triggers.reason: New public API, needs contract-first design

positive_triggers.input: I need to define the type contract between our frontend and backend teams
We're publishing an SDK — what's the right API surface?
positive_triggers.reason: SDK design is high-stakes public API

negative_triggers.reason: Private helper, not a public interface

negative_triggers.input: Write a one-off data migration script

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Design a REST API for our user management module'
expected_behavior: Agent identifies contract surface: endpoints, input/output shapes, errors, auth, rate limits, versioning
Agent writes contract spec FIRST (OpenAPI / type definitions)
Agent applies Hyrum's Law: generic names, no internal types leaked
Agent defines typed errors with semantic kinds (not just HTTP status codes)
Agent specifies validation at trust boundary
Agent includes version strategy (URL path / header / content type)
Agent reviews contract with peer BEFORE implementing handlers

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Define type contract between frontend and backend'
expected_behavior: Agent identifies which types cross the boundary
Agent avoids leaking internal types (DB row types, internal IDs)
Agent specifies validation contract (which fields are required, formats, ranges)

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
